2603.20586.title: MKA: Memory-Keyed Attention for Efficient Long-Context Reasoning
2603.20586.paper_summary: As long-context language modeling becomes increasingly important, the cost of maintaining and attending to large Key/Value (KV) caches grows rapidly, becoming a major bottleneck in both training and inference. While prior works such as Multi-Query Attention (MQA) and Multi-Latent Attention (MLA) reduce memory by sharing or compressing KV features, they often trade off representation quality or incur runtime overhead. We propose Memory-Keyed Attention (MKA), a hierarchical attention mechanism that integrates multi-level KV caches (local, session, and long-term) and learns to route attention across them dynamically. We further introduce Route-Fused MKA (FastMKA), a broadcast-routed variant that fuses memory sources before attention computation for improved efficiency. Experiments on different sequence lengths show that FastMKA achieves a favorable accuracy-efficiency trade-off: comparable perplexity to MLA while achieving up to 5x faster training throughput and 1.8x lower evaluation latency. These results highlight MKA as a practical and extensible framework for efficient long-context attention.
2603.20586.paper_summary_zh: 隨著長上下文語言建模變得越來越重要，維護和關注大型鍵/值（KV）快取的成本迅速上升，成為訓練和推理中的主要瓶頸。雖然先前的工作如多查詢注意力（MQA）和多潛在注意力（MLA）通過共享或壓縮KV特徵來減少內存，但它們通常會以降低表示質量或增加運行時開銷為代價。我們提出了記憶鍵注意力（MKA），這是一種層次化的注意力機制，整合了多級KV快取（本地、會話和長期）並學習在它們之間動態路由注意力。我們進一步介紹了路由融合MKA（FastMKA），這是一種廣播路由變體，在注意力計算之前融合記憶源以提高效率。在不同序列長度上的實驗顯示，FastMKA實現了良好的準確性與效率的權衡：與MLA相比，困惑度相當，同時訓練吞吐量提高了最多5倍，評估延遲降低了1.8倍。這些結果突顯了MKA作為一個實用且可擴展的高效長上下文注意力框架。
2603.20586.author: Dong Liu et.al.
2603.20586.authors: Dong Liu, Yanxuan Yu, Ben Lengerich, Ying Nian Wu
